input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

recent_thinks: [0-2] A white background features a logo with an orange circle and black samurai profile beside "SAMURAI KATANA-SHOP.NL". Centered text reads "Japanese Sword Specialist" above a list including "Japanese Iaito" and "Nihonto". A URL sits at the bottom. In the second frame, a semi-transparent grey circular overlay appears over the top list items, partially obscuring the text.

[2-4] The title card transitions to a shot of a Japanese sword resting horizontally on a beige, vertically striped surface. Initially framed within a circle, the view expands to show the full weapon, featuring a long, curved silver blade and a white handle detailed with black diamond patterns and a dark guard.

samples.output: <think>The silver curved blade and white handle featuring black diamond patterns remain stationary on the beige textured surface. New white text reading "Snake Katana - Hanwei Forge" appears overlaid near the top center of the frame, positioned directly above the sword.</think><action>silent</action>

An orange circle containing a black samurai silhouette sits to the left of the text "SAMURAI" in black capital letters. Below this, "KATANA-SHOP.NL" appears in orange text, centered on the screen. The sword is no longer visible.</think><action>silent</action>

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

compressed_segments.text: [0-2] A white background features the "SAMURAI KATANA-SHOP.NL" logo with an orange circle and black samurai profile. Centered text reads "Japanese Sword Specialist" above a list including "Japanese Iaito" and "Nihonto". A semi-transparent grey circle briefly overlays the list. [2-6] The view transitions to a curved silver sword with a white handle detailed with black diamond patterns on a beige surface. White text "Snake Katana - Hanwei Forge" appears above. [6-14] The sword shifts diagonally as the camera zooms in, focusing first on the handle and dark guard, then excluding the blade to highlight the white wrapping, and finally tightening on the black pommel cap against the textured background.

[14-18] The camera zooms in on the black pommel cap at the handle's base, showing a gold ring detail where the white wrapping meets the cap against a textured beige background. [18-22] The view pulls back and pans right, revealing the white cord wrapping covering black diamond-shaped ray skin patterns along the handle's length. [22-26] Focusing on the center, golden ornaments depicting a snake design are visible through the wrapping gaps, flanked by black ray skin textures. [26-28] The camera moves to the black, textured handguard and gold collar, ending with the silver blade edge extending right against the woven mat.

[28-30] The camera zooms in extremely close, blurring the black handguard and white handle wrapping. [30-32] Focus returns, revealing a black sculpted handguard, white cord wrapping with black diamond patterns, and a gold collar at the blade base on a woven mat. [32-36] The view shifts right to expose the handguard's openwork design and snake-like scales. [36-42] The camera pans and adjusts, highlighting the detailed scales of the black snake-sculpted handguard, the white cord wrapping on the left, and the gold collar with the silver blade base on the right against the beige mat.

[42-46] The camera pulls back to display the entire handle assembly on a beige woven mat. White cord wrapping features black diamond patterns and reveals gold snake ornaments in the center. A black, snake-shaped handguard sits at the right end near the gold collar and blade base, while the pommel is on the left. [46-52] White text overlays appear, reading "Full-Tang Tsuka" at the top and "Snake motifs gracing the kashira, fuchi and tsuba" at the bottom. The camera pans right along the handle, bringing the gold collar and base of the silver blade into clearer view next to the black guard. [52-56] The text overlays disappear completely. The camera zooms in closer on the black snake-textured handguard and gold collar. The white wrapped handle with black diamonds remains visible on the left, while the silver blade extends towards the right edge.

recent_thinks: [56-58] The camera zooms in closer to the sword's guard area, highlighting the black, snake-scaled texture of the handguard and the gold collar. The white wrapped handle remains on the left, and the silver blade base is visible on the right edge against the beige woven mat background.

[58-60] The camera pushes into an extreme close-up of the black, snake-sculpted handguard, dominating the frame. The white cord wrapping is visible only on the far left edge, while the gold collar appears below the guard. The focus softens as the lens nears the textured black metal against the beige woven mat background.

[60-62] The camera withdraws from the extreme close-up, restoring sharp focus to the black snake-textured handguard and gold collar. The white cord-wrapped handle featuring distinct black diamond patterns becomes fully visible on the left, while the silver blade base extends towards the far right edge over the beige woven mat background.

queries.question: What is the primary color of the cord wrapping on the sword handle? A. White B. Black C. Red D. Gold

samples.output: <think>The camera pans right along the sword, shifting focus from the black snake-shaped guard and gold collar to the silver blade. The white wrapped handle exits the frame on the left. The view settles on the blade's surface, highlighting the temper line and metallic sheen against the beige woven mat.</think><action>silent</action>

compressed_segments.text: [0-2] A white background features the "SAMURAI KATANA-SHOP.NL" logo with an orange circle and black samurai profile. Centered text reads "Japanese Sword Specialist" above a list including "Japanese Iaito" and "Nihonto". A semi-transparent grey circle briefly overlays the list. [2-6] The view transitions to a curved silver sword with a white handle detailed with black diamond patterns on a beige surface. White text "Snake Katana - Hanwei Forge" appears above. [6-14] The sword shifts diagonally as the camera zooms in, focusing first on the handle and dark guard, then excluding the blade to highlight the white wrapping, and finally tightening on the black pommel cap against the textured background. [14-18] The camera zooms in on the black pommel cap at the handle's base, showing a gold ring detail where the white wrapping meets the cap against a textured beige background. [18-22] The view pulls back and pans right, revealing the white cord wrapping covering black diamond-shaped ray skin patterns along the handle's length. [22-26] Focusing on the center, golden ornaments depicting a snake design are visible through the wrapping gaps, flanked by black ray skin textures. [26-28] The camera moves to the black, textured handguard and gold collar, ending with

[56-60] The camera zooms into an extreme close-up of a black, snake-sculpted handguard and gold collar, momentarily softening focus against a beige woven mat background. [60-62] The view withdraws to sharply reveal the white cord-wrapped handle featuring distinct black diamond patterns on the left and the silver blade base on the right. [62-64] The camera pans right, shifting focus from the black snake-shaped guard and gold collar to the silver blade, highlighting its temper line and metallic sheen as the handle exits the frame. [64-70] White text reading "Forged Folded K120C Powder Steel Blade" overlays the silver blade as the camera continues to track along the polished steel, displaying the wavy temper line and metallic finish against the mat background while the gold collar exits the left edge.

[70-72] A white text overlay vanishes, revealing a polished silver blade on a beige woven mat. [72-80] The camera pans right along the steel, showcasing the sharp edge, metallic reflections, and a distinct wavy temper line leading to the curved tip. [80-82] The view zooms out to display the entire sword, featuring a white wrapped handle with black diamond patterns on the left connected to the long, curved blade extending across the textured background.

[82-86] A sword with a white handle featuring black diamond wrapping is displayed horizontally. Initially, it possesses a long, curved silver blade resting on a beige woven mat, then transitions to a distinctively wavy, sinusoidal blade on a light wooden surface with swirling grain.
[86-96] The scene shifts to a close-up of a black, snake-shaped handguard held against green foliage. The camera zooms in on the coiled metal snake sculpture, revealing detailed scales and a head wrapping around the rim. The white cord-wrapped handle extends from the base, positioned next to a gold collar near the blade connection. The view pans along the guard's structure, highlighting the texture of the snake scales and the white wrapping against the blurred natural background. The camera focuses on the side profile of the coiled snake design and the intricate metalwork of the guard.

[96-108] The camera maintains a close-up focus on a sword hilt against a background of blurred green foliage. A white cord-wrapped handle is visible, positioned generally at the bottom or right, leading towards a rectangular gold fitting. Surrounding this fitting is a black handguard sculpted into the shape of a coiled snake, featuring detailed metal scales. The perspective shifts slightly throughout the segment, highlighting the curved metal structure of the guard, the ribbed texture of the white handle wrapping, and the metallic sheen of the gold collar. The view emphasizes the depth of the snake sculpture and the craftsmanship of the hilt components.

recent_thinks: [108-110] The camera pans up from the hilt assembly, showing the full sword with its white handle and black snake guard before focusing on the silver blade. The view highlights the polished steel surface and wavy temper line against the green foliage background, centering on the blade's metallic texture.

[110-112] The camera pans upwards along the length of the curved silver blade, moving away from the white-wrapped handle and black snake guard. The view focuses on the polished steel surface and the wavy temper line running along the edge, set against a backdrop of dense green bamboo leaves.

[112-114] The camera pulls back to reveal the entire sword held vertically against a background of dense green bamboo leaves. The white cord-wrapped handle featuring black diamond patterns is visible at the bottom, topped by a black, snake-shaped handguard. The polished silver blade curves upwards towards the right, displaying its metallic sheen and distinct temper line.

[114-116] The sword remains held vertically against green bamboo leaves. A circular overlay appears in the center, displaying the black scabbard lying horizontally on a beige mat. The white handle with black diamond patterns is visible on the left side of the circular inset image.

[116-118] The circular overlay vanishes as the scene transitions to the sword lying horizontally on a beige woven mat. The blade is now fully sheathed inside a long, black scabbard. The white handle with black diamond-patterned wrapping is visible on the left, connected to the black snake-shaped guard.

[118-120] The camera pulls back slightly from the sheathed sword resting horizontally on a beige woven surface. The black scabbard occupies the right side, connected to the white handle with black diamond wrapping on the left. The stationary weapon remains centered as more of the textured mat background enters the frame.

input.system:
You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).
